sourceMovie.title: Code of Silence
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Code of Silence (1985) as a action theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The action and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Substitute
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Code of Silence clicked for you, The Substitute (1996) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and crime, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Code of Silence?
faq.answer: Try The Substitute, Assassination Games, Run All Night—each shares DNA with Code of Silence while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Code of Silence?
faq.answer: Code of Silence (1985) is best described as Action, Crime, Drama, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Code of Silence worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 5.8/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
